PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 6 Sub-batch 6.2b-ii SHIPPED — Per Area Report Page 2 heatmaps UI layer (Leaflet client island + leaflet.heat dep + HeatLayer wrapper hook + Page 2 RSC composer + composer mount). Pure consumer of 6.2b-i's locked data contract (lawEnforcementEventLocations + monitoringEventLocations + pre-densified patrolTracks). No new decisions locked — 6.2b-ii is a clean consumer of the "Heatmap Renderer Choice" decision lock from 6.2b-i.

              NEW components/heat-layer.tsx (~75 LOC) — thin Client wrapper around L.heatLayer plugin. Uses useMap() from react-leaflet + useEffect to add/remove the heat layer on mount/unmount AND on prop change (cleanup prevents stacked duplicates across re-renders). Two variants 'events' (red gradient red-200→red-600 with radius=18 blur=22) + 'tracks' (blue gradient blue-200→blue-700 with radius=14 blur=20) drive separate palettes per the 6.2b-i decision lock guidance. `import "leaflet.heat";` for side-effect plugin registration extending the L namespace with .heatLayer. Early-return when points.length === 0 to avoid mounting an empty layer.

              NEW components/per-area-heatmap-map.tsx (~140 LOC) — Client island following the AreaCoverageMap pattern. MapContainer + TileLayer (OSM) + HeatLayer×2 (tracks underneath events for layering — blue base + red highlight overlay reads correctly on the funder PDF) + AutoFitBounds (collects all points events ∪ tracks, maps [lat,lon,weight] → [lat,lon], fitBounds with 16px padding when ≥2 points) + MapReadySignal (mirrors AreaCoverageMap's contract verbatim — double rAF paint flush, 8s safety timeout matching Puppeteer waitForFunction, window.__renderReady flip per locked "Coverage Report Page 2 Map Render Strategy" decision). Default center Mindoro 13.0/121.0 zoom 9 overridden when AutoFitBounds finds ≥2 points.

              NEW page-2-heatmaps.tsx (~225 LOC RSC composer) — section page-break-before:always with teal section header "Page 2 — Heatmaps — {area} — {dateRange}"; 110mm-tall heatmap-container with empty-state ("No event locations or patrol tracks to display.") when both event arrays AND patrolTracks are empty; dual gradient legend rendered server-side via LegendGradientBar component as static linear-gradient CSS bars (events left = red 5-stop gradient, tracks right = blue 5-stop gradient) to avoid hydration drift between RSC and Client; methodology footer explaining 250m densification + density interpretation. eventsToHeatTuples + tracksToHeatTuples helpers flatten PerAreaReportEventLocation[] / PerAreaReportPatrolTrack[] into HeatLatLng[] tuples — both law-enforcement + monitoring events render on the same red gradient since Page 1 already surfaces the LE/monitoring split via bar charts (intentional single visual category at the map layer).

              NEW __tests__/page-2-heatmaps.test.tsx (10 net new vitest cases). Top-of-file `vi.mock("../components/per-area-heatmap-map", () => ({ PerAreaHeatmapMap: () => null }));` per the react-leaflet 🔴 gotcha — leaflet imports `window` unconditionally at module load and crashes in node-side unit tests; mocking the client island lets us test the SSR shell. Cases: section title contains area + dateRange, page-break-before regex match, empty-state when no events + no tracks, container mounts when events alone present, container mounts when tracks alone present, both legend bars (events + tracks) always rendered, legend gradient stops are static server-side hex literals (#fecaca/#dc2626 for events, #bfdbfe/#1d4ed8 for tracks), methodology footer mentions 250 densification, mixed law-enforcement + monitoring events flatten cleanly, multiple patrol tracks concat cleanly.

              MODIFIED apps/web/package.json (+2 deps — leaflet.heat ^0.2.0 runtime + @types/leaflet.heat ^0.2.4 dev). MODIFIED per-area-report.tsx (+8 LOC — import + mount Page2Heatmaps after Page1EventAndPatrolSummary).

              NO new lessons.md entries — 6.2b-ii is a pure pattern application of the 6.2b-i decision lock's implementation guidance. NO new DECISIONS_LOG.md entries — 6.2b-ii consumes the locked "Heatmap Renderer Choice" verbatim.
